items.title: Trump makes his case for Iran war, saying it will end 'shortly' but more strikes are ahead
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump hailed the military’s “unstoppable” prowess in the war with Iran, saying in an address, it will end “shortly” without offering a timeline.
items.content: President Donald Trump hailed the military’s “unstoppable” prowess in the war with Iran, saying in an address, it will end “shortly” without offering a timeline.

items.title: Iran war's "core strategic objectives are nearing completion," Trump says
items.standfirst: Trump says he expects the U.S. war with Iran to end within several weeks despite unrelenting attacks from both sides and Iran's iron grip on the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Trump says he expects the U.S. war with Iran to end within several weeks despite unrelenting attacks from both sides and Iran's iron grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump says Iran war will wrap up "very shortly," but warns of more strikes in coming weeks
items.standfirst: President Trump predicted in a prime-time address that the U.S. will complete its military mission in Iran "very shortly," and said U.S. forces have achieved "overwhelming victories," but he did not offer a definitive timeline.
items.content: President Trump predicted in a prime-time address that the U.S. will complete its military mission in Iran "very shortly," and said U.S. forces have achieved "overwhelming victories," but he did not offer a definitive timeline.

CBS News' Ed O'Keefe remarks on the president's remarks and J.D. Simkins, editor-in-chief of the Military Times and Defense News, joins with analysis.

items.title: Trump pledges to 'finish the job' in Iran in national address
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump defended US operations in Iran Wednesday night, pledging to “finish the job” as military objectives near completion. Speaking amid rising oil prices, volatile markets and fresh Iranian missile attacks, Trump outlined his timeline for ending the conflict and pressed allies to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: These 4 astronauts are making history on flight around the moon

items.title: What's the status of the Artemis II rocket?

Derrick Pitts, chief astronomer at the Franklin Institute, joins CBS News to discuss and CBS News' Cristian Benavides and Kris Van Cleave report.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II Lifts Off in Landmark Moon Mission

items.title: Artemis II launches historic flyby lunar mission with diverse crew
items.standfirst: Four astronauts – three Americans and one Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years. The 32-storey rocket soared from Kennedy Space Center, marking the start of a decade-long plan for sustainable Moon exploration and a future landing near the south pole.
items.content: Four astronauts – three Americans and one Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years. The 32-storey rocket soared from Kennedy Space Center, marking the start of a decade-long plan for sustainable Moon exploration and a future landing near the south pole.

items.title: 'Like a camping trip, but everything needs to be attached'
items.standfirst: NASA is set to launch four astronauts as soon as Wednesday evening on a 10-day flight around the moon, marking the most ambitious U.S. space mission in decades. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with astronaut Jean-François Clervoy, Founder of Air Zero G, about what life is going to be like for astronauts on board the Artemis II. He says the mission capsule is a very confined space and the four astronauts "better get along with each other".
items.content: NASA is set to launch four astronauts as soon as Wednesday evening on a 10-day flight around the moon, marking the most ambitious U.S. space mission in decades. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with astronaut Jean-François Clervoy, Founder of Air Zero G, about what life is going to be like for astronauts on board the Artemis II. He says the mission capsule is a very confined space and the four astronauts "better get along with each other".

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Code Leak Revealed Unreleased Features

items.title: OpenAI Tops $850 Billion Valuation | Bloomberg Tech 4/1/2026
items.standfirst: Bloomberg’s Tim Stenovec discusses OpenAI’s recent mega-funding round that valued the company at $852 billion. Plus, Anthropic blames the accidental release of internal source code behind its Claude coding assistant on human error. And, it’s launch day for Artemis II as NASA prepares to send astronauts back to the moon’s vicinity. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: NASA Artemis II Is Part of the Latest Space Race
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis II is set to launch, returning astronauts to the moon’s vicinity for the first time in 50 years. Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow discusses the milestone, the role private firms have played, and what this means for US competition with China. He speaks to Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.standfirst: Case could determine future of century-old practice of infants being born in US automatically being granted citizenship.
items.content: Case could determine future of century-old practice of infants being born in US automatically being granted citizenship.

items.title: Trump attends birthright citizenship hearing at Supreme Court in historic first
items.standfirst: In a historic first, Donald Trump became the first US sitting president to attend oral arguments at the Supreme Court on Wednesday as it weighed his bid to restrict birthright citizenship. During more than two hours of questioning, justices from across the ideological spectrum assessed the policy’s legality, indicating that the court may be poised to block a key aspect of Trump’s immigration agenda.
items.content: In a historic first, Donald Trump became the first US sitting president to attend oral arguments at the Supreme Court on Wednesday as it weighed his bid to restrict birthright citizenship. During more than two hours of questioning, justices from across the ideological spectrum assessed the policy’s legality, indicating that the court may be poised to block a key aspect of Trump’s immigration agenda.

items.title: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
items.standfirst: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warningOne person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake that struck off Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves.The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an island in Indonesia’s North Maluku province. Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on acting Venezuelan President Delcy Rodríguez
items.standfirst: WASHINGTON — The U.S. on Wednesday lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, according to an Office of Foreign Assets Control entry on the Treasury Department website
items.content: WASHINGTON — The U.S. on Wednesday lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, according to an Office of Foreign Assets Control entry on the Treasury Department website

items.title: Trump administration lifts sanctions on Venezuela’s interim leader
items.standfirst: The US has lifted sanctions on Delcy Rodriguez, Venezuela’s interim president, in a significant shift following the ouster of Nicolas Maduro. The move comes as relations between Washington and Caracas begin to thaw, with Rodriguez taking steps to open the country’s energy sector to US companies after assuming power earlier this year.
items.content: The US has lifted sanctions on Delcy Rodriguez, Venezuela’s interim president, in a significant shift following the ouster of Nicolas Maduro. The move comes as relations between Washington and Caracas begin to thaw, with Rodriguez taking steps to open the country’s energy sector to US companies after assuming power earlier this year.

items.title: Trump claims Iran wants a ceasefire, Iran says remarks are ‘false and baseless’
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has claimed that Iran has asked the United States for a ceasefire -- adding that Washington would only consider it when the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Tehran, though, has denied this -- the Foreign Ministry calling Trump's remarks "baseless." It comes hours ahead of the president's primetime address to the nation. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

clusters.title: SpaceX Said to File Confidentially for IPO Before AI Rivals
clusters.neutral_headline: SpaceX Said to File Confidentially for IPO Before AI Rivals
bullet_summary: The company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public
75T, people familiar with the matter have said

items.title: SpaceX Said to File Confidentially for IPO Before AI Rivals
items.standfirst: SpaceX has filed confidentially for an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, bringing billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company closer to delivering the biggest-ever listing. The company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. A representative for SpaceX didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment. SpaceX could seek a valuation in the IPO of more than $1.75T, people familiar with the matter have said. The company acquired Musk’s artificial intelligence startup xAI in a deal that valued the enlarged entity at $1.25T. Bloomberg US Equity Capital Markets Reporter Anthony Hughes joins Paul Sweeney and Scarlet Fu on Bloomberg Intelligence to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: SpaceX has filed confidentially for an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, bringing billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company closer to delivering the biggest-ever listing. The company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. A representative for SpaceX didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment. SpaceX could seek a valuation in the IPO of more than $1.75T, people familiar with the matter have said. The company acquired Musk’s artificial intelligence startup xAI in a deal that valued the enlarged entity at $1.25T. Bloomberg US Equity Capital Markets Reporter Anthony Hughes joins Paul Sweeney and Scarlet Fu on Bloomberg Intelligence to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX confidentially files to go public at $1.75tn, reports say
items.standfirst: Elon Musk’s rocket company could go public as early as June, Bloomberg reportsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxSpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering on the US stock market, according to reports from Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal. The IPO is set to be one of the most closely watched and highly valued listings in market history.Elon Musk’s company, which has become a dominant power in both space travel and satellite communications, could potentially seek a valuation upwards of $1.75tn. The confidential filing will give regulators a period to review and discuss the company’s financial disclosures before investors and the public are able to view them. Continue reading...
items.content: Elon Musk’s rocket company could go public as early as June, Bloomberg reportsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxSpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering on the US stock market, according to reports from Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal. The IPO is set to be one of the most closely watched and highly valued listings in market history.Elon Musk’s company, which has become a dominant power in both space travel and satellite communications, could potentially seek a valuation upwards of $1.75tn. The confidential filing will give regulators a period to review and discuss the company’s financial disclosures before investors and the public are able to view them. Continue reading...
